speaker: Administrator Beton
description: Voice-reference dialogue samples for Administrator Beton, the Chapter 2 landlord representative in Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game text.
source_role_summary: Chapter 2 landlord-side NPC and field antagonist
Represents building management in Waldek's eviction/poster dispute
Controls access to Landlord's Office records, Building Rules, Warning Letters, Complaint Log, and management explanations
Rigidly invokes façade harmony, resident comfort, property standards, and management discretion
Provides pressure, evasive phrasing, and evidence contradictions for the Housing Court chapter
Not cartoon-evil: sincerely believes order, uniformity, and management control prevent chaos

voice_profile.core_voice: rigid, polished, procedural, defensive, euphemistic, officious, concrete-minded, dry by accident
comedy_engine: translates every human problem into building-management jargon
uses terms like façade harmony and resident comfort with excessive seriousness
cannot recognise how absurd the poster dispute sounds when said aloud
treats Waldek's home as a unit, a window, or an occupancy matter before remembering he is a person
becomes funnier when trying not to sound punitive
do: make him professionally plausible, not a moustache-twirling villain
make him genuinely committed to order and consistency, even when the record contradicts him
use bureaucratic euphemisms: necessary measure, management response, visual standard, resident comfort, escalation pathway
make his evasions create useful evidence trails
allow moments of discomfort when challenged with real home impact
do_not: make him anti-tenant as a personality only
make all his lines identical jargon

relationship_dynamics.Dr. A. Cula: treats him as inexperienced but increasingly irritatingly precise
relationship_dynamics.Waldek: speaks about him as tenant, occupant, or unit-holder until challenged
relationship_dynamics.Attorney Grzyb: relies on him for management facts but wishes he would use fewer dangerous phrases
relationship_dynamics.Zielinska: finds her comparator evidence inconvenient and calls it anecdotal
relationship_dynamics.Kowalski: tries to use him as disturbance evidence, then regrets the specificity of his non-complaints
relationship_dynamics.Crab: is unnerved by Crab's date scrutiny and complaint-log arithmetic
relationship_dynamics.Whimsy: dismisses Whimsy as rhetorical but accidentally gives him material
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Murrow: dislikes Murrow's structured evidence requests because they narrow his escape routes
relationship_dynamics.Asia: underestimates her file organisation, then discovers she preserved the letter sequence
relationship_dynamics.Housing Judge: tries to sound reasonable before the judge, but his own records undermine overreach
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Pig: finds Pig's maritime panic unprofessional but somehow less dangerous than Crab's quiet questions
relationship_dynamics.Old Woman: treats her as informal gossip until her neighbourhood memory matches the records
relationship_dynamics.Barista: dismisses café gossip but fears the notice wall comparison
relationship_dynamics.Archivist: fears archival version history more than cross-examination
